data.content:
Plan for terminate-hang bug (b71a / foolery-e0cc)

Observed behavior from repo review
- The terminate UI calls DELETE /api/terminal, which maps directly to terminal-manager abortSession().
- abortSession() immediately marks the session as aborted, but the actual teardown only sends SIGTERM to the tracked child and later SIGKILL if child.killed is still false.
- finishSession() currently overwrites the terminal status from exit code only (0 => completed, non-zero => error), so an aborted session can be re-labeled after the process exits.
- session-connection-manager also converts every exit event into completed/error/disconnected, so a locally aborted session is not preserved as aborted once exit arrives.
- Agent children are spawned as a single tracked process with no explicit process-group cleanup, so wrapper/descendant processes can survive terminate and keep producing output.

Likely root cause
- Terminate is not a first-class terminal outcome. The system mixes an optimistic UI/backend aborted status with a best-effort child kill, then later treats the eventual exit as a normal completion/error.
- If the agent command spawns descendants or ignores SIGTERM, the current kill path can fail to stop the real worker while the UI still thinks terminate succeeded.

Implementation plan
1. Update src/lib/terminal-manager.ts so termination is modeled explicitly.
   - Add per-session abort bookkeeping (for example abort requested / final status intent) instead of relying on exit code alone.
   - Ensure finishSession() preserves aborted as the terminal status and logs the interaction end consistently.
   - Cancel any pending stdin-close timers / follow-up loops on abort and make sure the active main child or take-loop child cannot continue scheduling new work after terminate.
2. Harden process teardown in src/lib/terminal-manager.ts.
   - Inspect whether the agent process should be spawned in its own process group and terminated via group kill so wrapper children do not escape.
   - Keep SIGTERM then SIGKILL fallback, but make the fallback key off real process liveness rather than child.killed, which only reports whether kill() was invoked.
   - Handle already-exited processes safely so repeated terminate clicks are idempotent.
3. Update client-side exit handling in src/lib/session-connection-manager.ts.
   - If a session is already marked aborted (or the backend reports it aborted), keep that status when exit is received instead of mapping to completed/error.
   - Keep notification text aligned with terminated semantics.
4. Validate the UI touchpoints in src/app/beats/page.tsx and src/components/terminal-panel.tsx.
   - Confirm both terminate entry points still update local state correctly and do not race the SSE exit handling.
   - Only adjust these files if needed for status or toast consistency after the backend/session-manager changes.

Test strategy
- Extend src/lib/__tests__/session-connection-manager.test.ts with a case where the store already shows aborted before exit arrives; expected result is status stays aborted and no success/error relabeling occurs.
- Add/extend terminal-manager tests (likely in src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-knots-next-guard.test.ts or a dedicated terminal-manager abort test) to cover:
  - abortSession() marking and preserving aborted on close
  - terminate during a running spawned child
  - terminate while a take-loop iteration is active so no follow-up iteration starts
- If process-group logic changes, include a mocked assertion that the kill path targets the intended pid/process-group and that repeated abort calls do not regress.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium.
- Main risk is cross-platform process-tree termination behavior. The implementation should prefer the safest Node-compatible approach used by this app environment and avoid regressing normal completed/error/disconnected flows.
